data.title: FIS raises quarterly dividend by 10% to $0.44 per common share, payable on March 24, 2026, for shareholders on record by March 10, 2026.
data.description: Financial technology company FIS has revealed a 10% hike in its regular quarterly dividend to $0.44 per common share. This increased dividend will be paid out on March 24, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 10, 2026. FIS, listed on the NYSE as FIS, is a leading provider of financial services technology to financial institutions, businesses, and developers, bridging the gap between financial technology and the global market.

data.title: Minerals Technologies Inc. (NYSE: MTX) reported Q4 2025 earnings per share of $1.19, or $1.27 excluding special items.
data.description: In New York on January 29, 2026, Minerals Technologies Inc. (NYSE: MTX) announced their quarterly earnings. The company, known for their specialty minerals and technology-driven approach, revealed that their earnings per share for the fourth quarter ending on December 31, 2025, were $1.19. However, excluding special items, this figure stood at $1.27.

data.title: HALPER SADEH LLC urges Cummins Inc. shareholders to discuss their rights. Contact firm as soon as possible.
data.description: Investors are urged to promptly get in touch with the company as there is a time constraint to assert their rights. On January 29, 2026, Halper Sadeh LLC, a legal firm focusing on investor rights, disclosed an examination into potential misconduct by specific officers and directors of Cummins Inc. (NYSE: CMI). It is crucial for shareholders to act swiftly to safeguard their interests.

data.title: HALPER SADEH LLC urges iRhythm Technologies, Inc. shareholders to discuss rights promptly. Investigating officers/directors as of Jan. 29, 2026.
data.description: Investors are advised to promptly get in touch with the company as time to protect their rights may be running out. On January 29, 2026, in New York, Halper Sadeh LLC, a legal firm specializing in investor rights, announced an examination into potential misconduct by specific officers and directors at iRhythm Technologies, Inc. under the symbol NASDAQ: IRTC.

data.title: Sagen MI Canada Inc. reports Q4 2025 net income of $162 million, down $0.6 million y-o-y. Dividend of $0.3375 per preferred share declared.
data.description: In Toronto on January 29, 2026, Sagen MI Canada Inc. (TSX: MIC) released their financial report for the fourth quarter of 2025, disclosing a net income of $162 million. This amount marked a $0.6 million decrease from the previous year's fourth quarter, mainly attributed to reduced investment income and higher insurance finance costs. However, this was partly offset by improved insurance service outcomes. The company's Board of Directors also declared a dividend of $0.3375 per Class A preferred share, Series 1, scheduled to be paid on March 31, 2026, to shareholders of record by March 13, 2026. Sagen MI Canada Inc. announced this update without specifying the source.
All-time high production and significant cash increase in the last quarter.

data.title: Ovintiv appoints Gregory P. Hill, 64, as independent board member effective Jan 30, 2026.
data.description: On January 29, 2026, Ovintiv Inc. (NYSE: OVV) (TSX: OVV) revealed that Gregory P. Hill, aged 64, has been designated as an independent member of its board of directors, starting from January 30, 2026. Hill, who retired in 2025 as President, Worldwide Exploration & Production, joins the board at Ovintiv to contribute his expertise and insights.

data.title: Law firm Halper Sadeh LLC urges Archer-Daniels-Midland shareholders to discuss their rights, calling for immediate action.
data.description: Investors are urged to promptly reach out to the company as there is a time constraint to exercise their rights. Taking place on Jan. 29, 2026 in New York, Halper Sadeh LLC, a legal firm specializing in protecting investor rights, is in the process of examining whether specific officers and directors of Archer-Daniels-Midland Company (NYSE) have engaged in wrongful actions.

data.title: Halper Sadeh LLC urges Amylyx Pharmaceuticals shareholders to act promptly to discuss potential rights issues.
data.description: Investors are advised to promptly reach out to the company as time may be restricted to protect their rights. A legal firm in New York, Halper Sadeh LLC, is currently looking into potential misconduct by specific executives and board members at Amylyx Pharmaceuticals, Inc. listed on the NASDAQ. It is necessary for shareholders to act swiftly in this matter.

data.title: Apple exceeds Q1 earnings with revenue of $143.76 billion, beating estimates of $138.42 billion, with earnings at $2.84 per share. Active installed base reaches over 2.5 billion.
data.description: Apple Inc reported its first-quarter financial results for fiscal 2026 after the market closed on Thursday. The tech giant exceeded analyst expectations, with revenue totaling $143.76 billion compared to estimates of $138.42 billion. Earnings per share stood at $2.84 for the quarter, surpassing estimates of $2.66. Year-over-year, revenue increased by 16% and earnings per share by 19%. Sales in the Americas reached $58.53 billion, Europe at $38.15 billion, Greater China around $25.53 billion, Japan at $9.4 billion, and the rest of Asia Pacific at $12.14 billion. Apple stock is currently trading near its recent peak. Further insights on AAPL stock's future can be found on Benzinga.com.
BERTELSMANN IS SAID TO CONSIDER BUYING CONCORD MUSIC

data.title: Former executives of First Brands Group indicted by US DOJ; CEO and SVP charged. VP of Finance pleads guilty. #CorporateNews
data.description: First Brands Group, LLC released a statement announcing the indictment of former CEO Patrick James and former Senior VP Edward James by the U.S. Department of Justice, alongside the guilty plea of ex-VP of Finance Peter Andrew Brumbergs. The charges stem from past activities by specific individuals within the company.

data.title: Mercedes S-Class L4 Robotaxi powered by NVIDIA DRIVE AV, aiming for premium robotaxi deployment with Uber.
data.description: Mercedes-Benz introduces their latest S-Class model equipped with NVIDIA DRIVE AV L4 software and Hyperion architecture. The collaboration aims to focus on premium robotaxi deployment in partnership with Uber.
Perplexity inks Microsoft cloud deal amid dispute with Amazon. $AMZN $MSFT

data.title: Richard Lewis to succeed Tod Carpenter as President and CEO of Donaldson Company on March 2, 2026, after Carpenter's 30-year career.
data.description: Minneapolis-based company Donaldson Company, Inc. (NYSE: DCI) has appointed Richard Lewis as the new President and CEO, taking over from Tod Carpenter on March 2, 2026. Carpenter will become the Executive Chairman after serving as the President and CEO for the past 11 years and having a 30-year tenure with the company. Donaldson is a global leader in filtration products, with Lewis stepping into his new role as COO.

data.title: Minerals Technologies Inc. reported Q4 2025 net sales of $520M, operating income $62M, cash flow $64M, and full-year net sales of $2.07B, with operating income of $47M. More info at www.mineralstech.com.
data.description: Minerals Technologies Inc. reported earnings per share of $1.19 for Q4 2025, or $1.27 excluding special items. The company's worldwide net sales for the quarter were $520 million, with operating income at $62 million. Cash flow from operations was $64 million and free cash flow was $32 million. The full year 2025 net sales were $2.07 billion, with reported operating income of $47 million and a reported loss per share of $0.59. Consumer & Specialties segment sales were $1.10 billion, with segment reported operating income at $124 million. Engineered Solutions segment sales were $975 million, with segment reported operating income at $170 million. For the full year, cash flow from operations was $194 million, and free cash flow was $87 million. The company returned $73 million to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. MTI has 4,000 employees in 34 countries and reported global sales of $2.1 billion in 2025.

data.title: Transit Technologies names Cristina Wheless as COO to lead growth. Wheless brings extensive experience in transportation.
data.description: In Knoxville, Tennessee, on January 29, 2026, Transit Technologies, a prominent company in mobility software solutions, revealed the selection of Cristina Wheless as their latest Chief Operating Officer (COO). Wheless brings vast experience in advancing transportation services and will be key in the company's expansion.

data.title: Koi Computers renews ISO 9001:2015 certification, maintaining quality standards for federal government HPC solutions.
data.description: In Chicago on January 29, 2026, Koi Computers, a top provider of complete HPC solutions to the federal government, announced the successful renewal of its ISO 9001:2015 certification. This renewal exemplifies Koi Computers' dedication to upholding a robust quality management system.
